lenses.guidance: Two paired sections: Bezalel's Spirit-gifting (vv 1-11) sets up tabernacle-craft, then the Sabbath sign (vv 12-17) frames the work with rest. v 18's tablets close the unit. The literary structure subordinates work to covenant-rest.

Bezalel 'filled with the Spirit of God' (v 3) is the OT's first explicit Spirit-filling formula. The pattern anticipates Pentecost's craftsmen-of-the-gospel filled to build a different sanctuary — the church as living tabernacle.

vv 12-17 calls Sabbath 'a sign forever' (v 17) — the rhythm marks Israel as God's. The practical pattern: trust that the work continues without you. To rest in obedience here is to confess that Yahweh, not Israel, builds the tabernacle.
